channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: Breaking: NDAs to be waived for landmark military sexual violence inquiry
item.description: Gag orders imposed on former and current Defence personnel who experienced sexual violence will not be enforced, allowing them to participate in a landmark military sexual violence inquiry.
item.dc:creator: Laura Lavelle

media:group.media:description: There were fears some of the worst-affected veterans would be barred from giving evidence to the inquiry.

item.title: Race to develop Ebola vaccine as deadly strain of virus spreads
item.description: Nearly 250 people are suspected to have died, and more than 1,100 are infected with the rare strain of Ebola in the DRC and Uganda, though the true spread of the virus is feared to be much wider.

media:group.media:description: Efforts are ramping up to rapidly develop a vaccine for the rare strain of Ebola driving a deadly outbreak.

item.title: French Open Sabalenka's for the taking as draw opens up for number one
item.description: A series of upsets across Roland Garros leaves world number one Aryna Sabalenka in the box seat to win the claycourt grand slam after beating Naomi Osaka.

media:group.media:description: Aryna Sabalenka is one of the only players left in the draw with second-week slam experience.

item.title: Live: Wall Street closes at record highs, Australia's minimum wage rise announced today
item.description: Wall Street's S&P 500 closed at a record, but our market doesn't appear set to follow. A decision on the size minimum wage increase will be announced this morning. Track the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.
item.dc:creator: Daniel Ziffer

media:group.media:description: ASX Board. Bridge st Sydney

item.title: Farmers flock to buy CCTV cameras to stop fuel theft
item.description: Fuel theft is prompting farmers to install CCTV cameras at unprecedented rates, leading one security company to overhaul its entire business model.
item.dc:creator: Jackson Byrne

media:group.media:description: Dale Farley says he suspects people have been stealing diesel from his property for years.

item.title: Victorian government promises 'tougher scrutiny' of IVF clinics
item.description: The government is introducing amendments to laws governing IVF clinics that will allow the state's health minister to personally intervene to cancel a provider's registration.

media:group.media:description: The Victorian government is introducing amendments to the state's IVF legislation.

item.title: Live: Fair Work Commission to hand down annual wage review
item.description: Almost 3 million workers will learn how much their wages will increase when the Fair Work Commission hands down its annual review today. Follow live.
item.dc:creator: Courtney Gould

media:group.media:description: Treasurer Jim Chalmers addresses reporters in Canberra on 27 May 2026.

item.title: Victorian watchdog set up to monitor farmland restoration amid mineral sands boom
item.description: Mineral sands projects in the Wimmera Mallee will be assessed by a new governance group, to ensure land restoration plans are appropriate.
item.dc:creator: Wade Stephens

media:group.media:description: A new working group will keep a watchful eye on how mining companies operate in small western Victoria towns.

item.title: Flapping drone mimicking peregrine falcon gives hope to strawberry growers
item.description: A drone with flapping wings is being trialled to scare away rainbow lorikeets that are eating hundreds of thousands of dollars' worth of strawberries.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Nichols

media:group.media:description: Harry Lagastes and Jill Viccars with a RoBird drone.

item.title: In this PFAS-affected town, locals still can't eat too many fish from the river
item.description: Residents in Katherine have welcomed a $2 billion lawsuit over PFAS contamination in the town, saying "it's about time we started holding large corporations to account".
item.dc:creator: James Elton

media:group.media:description: Anjali Palmer says she's pleased the government is taking 3M to court over PFAS contamination.

item.title: 'Excited to be back': Serena Williams announces return to pro-tennis at 44
item.description: The greatest women's champion of the Open Era is returning to tennis after almost four years away, accepting a wildcard into the doubles at the Queen's Club tournament next week.

media:group.media:description: Serena Williams last played professional tennis in 2022.

item.title: 'A fifth of the price': Council uses AI for 10-year strategic plan
item.description: A Tasmanian council says using AI for a strategic plan has saved them time and money, and will be used much more in the future, but a resident says the community consultation is 'completely lost with AI'.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie Archer

media:group.media:description: West Tamar Council takes in the city of Launceston.

item.title: Ex-soldier, comedian and aspiring Martian is the Aussie face of Laos cave rescue
item.description: Josh Richards has made news for his diving and his interplanetary ambitions. Now he's using his skills to try to rescue others.
item.dc:creator: Josh Brine

media:group.media:description: Josh Richards has made a name for himself by squeezing through tight restrictions.

item.title: Julie's life was in disarray. Then she found a safe haven in Sydney
item.description: A centuries-old mansion in Sydney became Julie's safe haven, following a series of difficult life circumstances. She is one of many women being assisted through meanwhile use housing.
item.dc:creator: Tamsyn Buckle

media:group.media:description: Julie found solace at Mosman House after experiencing hardship during the COVID-19 pandemic.

item.title: How bridge named after Paul Pisasale spiralled into a four-year dispute
item.description: What began as an argument over Paul Pisasale's name on a local bridge evolved into allegations, tribunal hearings and political infighting. A deputy mayor has now been found guilty of misconduct.
item.dc:creator: Tobi Loftus

media:group.media:description: Former Ipswich mayor Paul Pisasale was found guilty of extortion in 2019.

item.title: Tough times for hospitality businesses with rising cost of food and power
item.description: The hospitality sector is grappling with "unprecedented" cost increases, according to a national peak body, with many being forced to close their doors.
item.dc:creator: Rachael Merritt

media:group.media:description: Simone Douglas says the cost of running a hospitality service has skyrocketed.

item.title: Real estate agency using fake AI salesperson to spam customers
item.description: "Daniel" is an AI real estate agent who calls strangers and secretly records conversations without their consent.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Fanning and Kenji Sato

media:group.media:description: Ellen Fanning received a phone call from "Daniel", who turned out to be an AI system.

item.title: Former wife of tobacco baron alleges horrific life entangled in illegal trade
item.description: After an arranged marriage to tobacco baron Sam Albion, Kawther Salih says the relationship spiralled into an abusive situation that saw her subjugated into working in the underground tobacco industry.
item.dc:creator: Liam Walsh and Niddal Mustafa

media:group.media:description: Kawther Salih says she was subjugated into working in the underground tobacco industry.

item.title: Neighbours complain about 200 chopper flights a week into luxury resort
item.description: A luxury resort that has been shut for three years hopes to reopen by chauffeuring guests down a steep 4WD track and flying them in on more than 200 helicopter flights a week.
item.dc:creator: Lani Oataway

media:group.media:description: Emirates's Wolgan Valley resort hopes to increase its helicopter flights.

item.title: Regional voters consider their options as One Nation popularity surges
item.description: Pauline Hanson's right-wing populist party is leading opinion polls for the first time, and regional Queenslanders say they can see why.
item.dc:creator: Ellie Willcox and Emily Dobson

media:group.media:description: Jake Tanti is considering his options, but says One Nation has dominated his social media algorithm.

item.title: Antibiotic found in wild seafood 14 kilometres from salmon farm
item.description: Tasmania's Natural Resources and Environment Department has confirmed the antibiotic florfenicol was detected at a 'low level' in wild seafood 14 kilometres from where the drug was administered.
item.dc:creator: Loretta Lohberger and Jano Gibson

media:group.media:description: The salmon industry was granted an emergency licence to use florfenicol in November last year, but the permit was revoked in March.

item.title: Cats eradicated from Little Dog Island
item.description: Penguins have been sighted on a remote Bass Strait island for the first time in decades after the successful eradication of feral cats and possums.
item.dc:creator: Hayden Smith

media:group.media:description: Kalinka, one of the detection dogs used during the monitoring program.

item.title: Parent 'thought it was a joke' upon hearing year 3 NAPLAN tests stolen
item.description: Dozens of primary school students are victims of an analogue data breach after their NAPLAN tests were stolen from a van in Melbourne's inner-north on April Fool's Day.
item.dc:creator: Danny Tran

media:group.media:description: NAPLAN test papers of dozens of school students from Melbourne's inner-north are missing.

item.title: What the polls reveal about One Nation's growing appeal
item.description: The One Nation leader, whose popularity has soared in the past 12 months, would be the first to say she doesn’t put too much stock in the numbers.
item.dc:creator: Clare Armstrong

media:group.media:description: One Nation's primary vote continues to climb and now leads against both major parties.

item.title: The impact of Australia only buying second-hand submarines under AUKUS
item.description: Australia will only buy second-hand Virginia nuclear-powered submarines from the US during the AUKUS stopgap phase, with some analysts criticising the move and others believing it is a positive change of plan.
item.dc:creator: Annika Burgess

media:group.media:description: Australia will be buying three used Virginia-class submarines as part of the stopgap phase of AUKUS.
